project.name: RUDP
project.type: experimental C++23 static library plus demo executable
project.primary_goal: Implement a lightweight reliable UDP protocol with channel-specific delivery semantics and a shared connection lifecycle.
project.status_summary: Prototype / incomplete implementation. Core packet encode/decode, session orchestration, handshake skeleton, selective ACK state, and some close/reset handling exist. Many protocol behaviors remain partial.

verified_locally.cmake_configure_build_tests: fails because GTestConfig.cmake is not installed in the current environment

readme.summary: High-level overview, motivation, design goals, and rough project structure.

protocol_spec.summary: Detailed draft protocol specification for packet format, flags, reliability semantics, handshake, retransmission, and channel behavior.

architecture_note.summary: Small mermaid diagram showing UDP datagram -> codec -> session -> TX/RX/channel/lifecycle paths.

public_headers.responsibility: Protocol constants, flags, channel types, packet header, PacketView, and serial-number comparison helpers.
Endian helpers for reading/writing 32-bit and 64-bit integers from byte spans.
Header validation plus packet encode/decode between Header/PacketView and byte datagrams.
Session state model, TX/RX sub-state, connection states, send requests, retained packets, inflight retransmission metadata, and emitted session events.
Outgoing path: queueing app data, applying remote ACK state, selecting handshake/control/retransmit/fresh packets.
Incoming path: control flag handling, reliable receive window updates, and channel-specific delivery/event generation.
Facade API for users: queue_send, poll_tx, on_datagram_received, request_close, drain_events, and role/connection state introspection.

notes: No visible bounds checking; callers must provide valid spans.

source_files.responsibility: Packet serialization/deserialization and basic header validation.
notes: decode returns PacketView with payload span referencing caller-provided bytes.
Header validation checks header_len, reserved byte, channel type range, and top 2 flag bits.

source_files.responsibility: Coordinates TxHandler and RxHandler. Applies incoming ACK data to TX state before handing packet to RX path. Converts rx.should_ack into tx.ack_only_pending.
notes: Acts as orchestration layer; some lifecycle policy still bleeds into handlers.

source_files.responsibility: Queues app data, tracks inflight reliable/control packets, consumes remote ACK/AckBits, emits handshake/control/retransmission/fresh packets.
notes: Client auto-starts handshake on first poll_tx when in Closed state.
Handshake/control packets that consume reliable sequence numbers are inserted into inflight map.
request_close currently triggers FIN emission through fin_pending + Closing state.
Pure ACK packets are sent as unreliable channel_type with seq=0.

source_files.responsibility: Handles control flags, connection state transitions, reliable receive window tracking, and delivery per channel type.
notes: Transitions connection_state on SYN / ACK / FIN / RST.
Schedules tx.syn_ack_pending and tx.final_ack_pending for handshake responses.
Pushes Connected / ConnectionClosed / ConnectionReset / DataReceived events into rx.pending_events.

source_files.responsibility: Minimal demo stub that only prints a message.

coverage: Established connection receives data then reset
Graceful close request leading to FIN
notes: Tests were authored but not executed in the current environment because GTest is missing.

codec: Decode bytes into Header plus borrowed payload span
reliability: TX inflight map keyed by sequence number
Remote cumulative ACK / AckBits application removes acknowledged inflight packets
Receive path tracks next_expected and received_bits for reliable/control packets
Retransmit on timeout using simple fixed initial RTO
handshake: Client auto-sends SYN from Closed on first poll_tx
Server receives SYN and schedules SYN|ACK
Client receives SYN|ACK, enters Established, schedules final ACK
Server receives final handshake ACK and enters Established
Connected event emitted when state transitions into Established
closing_and_reset: request_close on Established moves local state to Closing and schedules FIN
Receiving FIN moves local state to Closing and emits ConnectionClosed event
Receiving RST moves local state to Reset and emits ConnectionReset event
Receiver schedules ACK-only response on control packets

channels.ReliableOrdered: Packet retained in ordered_reorder_buffer, but ordered delivery drain is not implemented.
channels.ReliableUnordered: Immediately emits DataReceived event; duplicate suppression is not implemented.
channels.MonotonicState: Parses first 4 payload bytes as version and only emits when version is newer by signed serial-number comparison.

known_gaps.description: Protocol flag layout was previously drifting between spec and code; verify any future changes keep ACK/SYN-ACK encoding aligned.
Exact receive-side Ack/AckBits semantics are incomplete; old/duplicate/out-of-order reliable packets are not fully modeled.
Reliable ordered reorder buffer exists but contiguous drain / in-order event delivery is not implemented.
Reliable unordered packets are delivered immediately without duplicate suppression.
FIN/RST handling is partial. There is no full bidirectional close state machine, no terminal closed-after-fin path, and no linger semantics.
Fast retransmit based on AckBits gap detection is not implemented.
Retransmission timeout/backoff policy is fixed and simplistic.
Client handshake starts implicitly on first poll_tx while Closed. This may not be the desired API boundary for a production design.
Connection identifier exists in Header but there is no allocation, negotiation, validation, or session binding logic.
SessionEvent::Error exists but is effectively unused.
src/main.cpp is only a print stub and does not exercise the library.
Unit tests exist but cannot currently be configured locally due missing GTest package.

inline_todos_present_in_code: Implement fast retransmit using AckBits gap detection
Implement exact reliable ACK window update semantics per spec
Implement ordered reliable reorder and contiguous drain
Implement reliable unordered duplicate suppression policy

usage_pattern: Caller creates Session as client or server
Caller periodically polls poll_tx(now_ms) and sends returned datagram over UDP if present
Caller feeds received UDP datagrams into on_datagram_received(bytes, now_ms)
Caller drains events to consume application-visible notifications

coupling_and_design_observations: Session is a thin facade but still contains lifecycle glue by converting rx.should_ack into tx.ack_only_pending after every received packet.
TxHandler and RxHandler both mutate parts of the shared state model and are coupled through scheduling flags such as syn_ack_pending, final_ack_pending, fin_pending, and ack_only_pending.
Connection state is stored at SessionState level but passed by reference into handlers rather than being encapsulated in a dedicated state machine object.
Control flow priority in TxHandler is handshake/control first, then retransmit, then ack-only, then fresh app data.
PacketView payload lifetime is borrowed; higher-level code must not persist it without copying. RxHandler already copies when storing or emitting data.

likely_refactor_axes.name: Extract explicit connection state machine
likely_refactor_axes.motivation: Handshake, reset, and close logic is currently spread across TxHandler, RxHandler, and Session.

likely_refactor_axes.name: Separate control-plane and data-plane packet generation
likely_refactor_axes.motivation: TxHandler currently handles handshake, close, retransmission, pure ACK, and app data in one priority chain.

likely_refactor_axes.name: Formalize reliable receive window
likely_refactor_axes.motivation: RX Ack/AckBits semantics, duplicate handling, and ordered delivery are only partially implemented.

likely_refactor_axes.name: Resolve protocol/spec drift before deeper changes
likely_refactor_axes.motivation: The bit layout inconsistency between Protocol.md and Protocol.hpp can invalidate tests and future design decisions.

likely_refactor_axes.name: Clarify Session public API lifecycle
likely_refactor_axes.motivation: The current API implicitly starts client handshake on poll_tx and implicitly uses request_close for FIN; a higher-level design may want explicit connect/accept/close APIs.

likely_refactor_axes.name: Improve testability and determinism
likely_refactor_axes.motivation: Handlers are currently time-driven via now_ms and hidden state mutations; more explicit command/result objects would simplify tests.

suggested_questions_for_refactoring_mode: Should connection lifecycle become its own state machine class, separate from channel delivery and ACK bookkeeping?
Should the public Session API expose explicit connect/accept/close operations instead of implicit handshake-on-poll?
How should control packets, ACK-only packets, and app-data packets be prioritized and scheduled?
Should reliable ordered and reliable unordered delivery share one receive-tracking subsystem or separate policies over a common sequence window?
What is the cleanest way to reconcile the protocol specification with the current code flag layout?
Should PacketView borrowing remain in the public codec API, or should decode return an owning packet type for safer downstream use?
